120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234	
235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297

13	def get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(
14	    tokens: torch.Tensor,
15	    vocab_size: int,
16	    num_seqs: int,
17	) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
18	    # Compute the bin counts for the tokens.
19	    # vocab_size + 1 for padding.
20	    bin_counts = torch.zeros((num_seqs, vocab_size + 1),
21	                             dtype=torch.long,
22	                             device=tokens.device)
23	    bin_counts.scatter_add_(1, tokens, torch.ones_like(tokens))
24	    bin_counts = bin_counts[:, :vocab_size]
25	    mask = bin_counts > 0
26	
27	    return bin_counts, mask

30	def apply_penalties(logits: torch.Tensor, prompt_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,
31	                    output_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,
32	                    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor,
33	                    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor,
34	                    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
35	    """
36	    Applies penalties in place to the logits tensor
37	    logits : The input logits tensor of shape [num_seqs, vocab_size]
38	    prompt_tokens_tensor: A tensor containing the prompt tokens. The prompts 
39	        are padded to the maximum prompt length within the batch using 
40	        `vocab_size` as the padding value. The value `vocab_size` is used 
41	        for padding because it does not correspond to any valid token ID 
42	        in the vocabulary.
43	    output_tokens_tensor: The output tokens tensor.
44	    presence_penalties: The presence penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
45	    frequency_penalties: The frequency penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
46	    repetition_penalties: The repetition penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
47	    """
48	    num_seqs, vocab_size = logits.shape
49	    _, prompt_mask = get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(prompt_tokens_tensor,
50	                                                   vocab_size, num_seqs)
51	    output_bin_counts, output_mask = get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(
52	        output_tokens_tensor, vocab_size, num_seqs)
53	
54	    # Apply repetition penalties as a custom op
55	    from vllm._custom_ops import apply_repetition_penalties
56	    apply_repetition_penalties(logits, prompt_mask, output_mask,
57	                               repetition_penalties)
58	
59	    # We follow the definition in OpenAI API.
60	    # Refer to https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/parameter-details
61	    logits -= frequency_penalties.unsqueeze(dim=1) * output_bin_counts
62	    logits -= presence_penalties.unsqueeze(dim=1) * output_mask
63	    return logits

66	def default_unquantized_gemm(layer: torch.nn.Module,
67	                             x: torch.Tensor,
68	                             weight: torch.Tensor,
69	                             bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None):
70	    return torch.nn.functional.linear(x, weight, bias)

73	def rocm_unquantized_gemm(layer: torch.nn.Module,
74	                          x: torch.Tensor,
75	                          weight: torch.Tensor,
76	                          bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None):
77	    from vllm.platforms.rocm import on_gfx9
78	    k = weight.shape[1]

87	    n = x_view.shape[0]
88	    m = weight.shape[0]
89	    cu_count = current_platform.get_cu_count()
90	
91	    if m > 8 and 0 < n <= 4:
92	        out = ops.wvSplitK(weight, x_view, cu_count)
93	        return out.view(*x.shape[:-1], weight.shape[0])
94	    elif m % 4 == 0 and n == 1 and k <= 8192:
95	        out = ops.LLMM1(weight, x_view, 4)
96	        return out.view(*x.shape[:-1], weight.shape[0])

10	def apply_all_penalties(
11	    logits: torch.Tensor,
12	    prompt_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
13	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor,
14	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor,
15	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor,
16	    output_token_ids: list[list[int]],
17	) -> torch.Tensor:
18	    """
19	    Applies presence, frequency and repetition penalties to the logits.
20	    """
21	    _, vocab_size = logits.shape
22	    output_tokens_t = _convert_to_tensors(output_token_ids, vocab_size,
23	                                          logits.device)
24	    return apply_penalties(logits, prompt_token_ids, output_tokens_t,
25	                           presence_penalties, frequency_penalties,
26	                           repetition_penalties)
27	
28	
29	def _convert_to_tensors(output_token_ids: list[list[int]], vocab_size: int,
30	                        device: torch.device) -> torch.Tensor:
31	    """
32	    Convert the different list data structures to tensors.
33	    """
34	    output_tokens_tensor = make_tensor_with_pad(
35	        output_token_ids,
36	        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
37	        # token_id of this value.
38	        pad=vocab_size,
39	        device="cpu",
40	        dtype=torch.int64,
41	        pin_memory=is_pin_memory_available(),
42	    )
43	    return output_tokens_tensor.to(device, non_blocking=True)
44

22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234	
235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297

771	
772	    Args:

774	                        where N is the no. of tokens and M is the vocab dim.
775	        indices (torch.Tensor): List of chosen token indices.
776

778	        torch.Tensor: 1D tensor of shape (N,) where N is the no. of tokens.
779	                    Each element in the returned tensor represents the rank
780	                    of the chosen token in the input logprob tensor.
781	    """

783	             indices]

785	    del vals

169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
187	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
188	    """
189	
190	    def __init__(self):
191	        super().__init__()
192	
193	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
194	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
195	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
196	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
197	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
198	
199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document:

## Summary: vLLM SamplingParams Structure and Parameter Definitions

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase and gathered comprehensive information about the SamplingParams structure. Here's what I found:

### 1. **File Location**
- **Main file**: `/vllm/sampling_params.py` (lines 118-603)
- **Supporting file**: `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`
- **OpenAI API integration**: `/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

### 2. **Complete SamplingParams Class Definition**

The `SamplingParams` class is defined at **lines 118-589** in `sampling_params.py` as a `msgspec.Struct`. Here are all the parameters:

#### **Core Sampling Parameters:**
```python
n: int = 1                              # Number of output sequences
best_of: Optional[int] = None           # Best-of-N sampling
temperature: float = 1.0                # Temperature for sampling randomness
top_p: float = 1.0                      # Nucleus (cumulative probability) sampling
top_k: int = 0                          # Top-K sampling (0 means all tokens)
min_p: float = 0.0                      # Minimum probability threshold
```

#### **Penalty Parameters:**
```python
presence_penalty: float = 0.0           # Penalty for new tokens (appears in text)
frequency_penalty: float = 0.0          # Penalty based on token frequency
repetition_penalty: float = 1.0         # Overall repetition penalty
```

#### **Token Generation Control:**
```python
max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16          # Maximum tokens to generate
min_tokens: int = 0                     # Minimum tokens before EOS/stop
seed: Optional[int] = None              # Random seed
ignore_eos: bool = False                # Continue after EOS token
```

#### **Stop Sequences:**
```python
stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None     # Stop strings
stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None       # Stop token IDs
include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False         # Include stops in output
```

#### **Bad Words:**
```python
bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None           # Words to prohibit
_bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None  # Computed token IDs
```

#### **Detokenization & Token Handling:**
```python
detokenize: bool = True                         # Detokenize output
skip_special_tokens: bool = True                # Skip special tokens
spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True      # Add spaces between special tokens
```

#### **Probability Output:**
```python
logprobs: Optional[int] = None          # Number of log probabilities per token
prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None   # Log probabilities for prompt tokens
```

#### **Prompt Handling:**
```python
truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
```

#### **Output Format:**
```python
output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE
output_text_buffer_length: int = 0  # Internal: buffer for stop string evaluation
```

#### **Advanced Processing:**
```python
logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None         # Custom logit processors
guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None  # Guided generation
logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None  # Token logit biases
allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None  # Whitelist of tokens
extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None    # Custom extension parameters
```

#### **Internal Fields (Set After Init):**
```python
_real_n: Optional[int] = None           # Actual N value (when best_of is used)
_all_stop_token_ids: set[int]           # All stop tokens (computed in post_init)
```

### 3. **Validation Logic**

The validation occurs in the `_verify_args()` method (**lines 387-462**). Here's the complete validation:

| Parameter | Validation Rule |
|-----------|-----------------|
| `n` | Must be int, >= 1 |
| `best_of` | If set: must be int >= 1, and >= n |
| `presence_penalty` | Must be in [-2, 2] |
| `frequency_penalty` | Must be in [-2, 2] |
| `repetition_penalty` | Must be > 0 |
| `temperature` | Must be >= 0 |
| `top_p` | Must be in (0, 1] |
| `top_k` | Must be 0, -1, or >= 1; must be int |
| `min_p` | Must be in [0, 1] |
| `max_tokens` | If set, must be >= 1 |
| `min_tokens` | Must be >= 0, and <= max_tokens |
| `logprobs` | If set, must be >= 0 |
| `prompt_logprobs` | If set, must be >= 0 |
| `truncate_prompt_tokens` | Must be >= 1 |
| `stop_token_ids` | Must be list of ints only |
| `stop` | Cannot contain empty strings; requires detokenize=True |
| `best_of` vs `output_kind` | If best_of != n, cannot use DELTA output_kind |

### 4. **Special Validation Handling**

**In `__post_init__` (lines 327-385):**

1. **Temperature normalization**: If temperature < 1e-5, it's treated as zero (greedy sampling), and overrides are applied:
   - `top_p` → 1.0
   - `top_k` → 0
   - `min_p` → 0.0

2. **Temperature bounds checking**: If 0 < temperature < 1e-2, it's bumped up to 1e-2 to prevent numerical errors.

3. **Stop sequence normalization**:
   - Single stop string converted to list
   - Empty list if None

4. **Log probability conversion**: `True` values converted to `1`

5. **Seed handling**: `-1` seed converted to `None`

6. **Stop token tracking**: All stop tokens collected into `_all_stop_token_ids`

### 5. **Parameter Ranges and Defaults**

```python
_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5   # Epsilon for comparing floats to zero
_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2       # Minimum allowed temperature

# Default values (from ChatCompletionRequest):
temperature: 1.0
top_p: 1.0
top_k: 0
min_p: 0.0
repetition_penalty: 1.0
presence_penalty: 0.0
frequency_penalty: 0.0
```

### 6. **Key Methods**

- `from_optional()`: Factory method with optional parameters
- `__post_init__()`: Initialization validation and setup
- `_verify_args()`: Comprehensive parameter validation
- `_verify_greedy_sampling()`: Additional checks for greedy mode
- `update_from_generation_config()`: Updates from model config
- `update_from_tokenizer()`: Converts bad_words to token IDs
- `clone()`: Deep copy with special handling for LogitsProcessor
- `sampling_type` property: Returns SamplingType (GREEDY, RANDOM, RANDOM_SEED)

### 7. **Sampling Types** (from `SamplingType` enum)

```python
class SamplingType(IntEnum):
    GREEDY = 0           # temperature < 1e-5
    RANDOM = 1           # No seed
    RANDOM_SEED = 2      # With seed
```

### 8. **Relation to Your Top-A Interest**

While vLLM doesn't currently have a direct `top_a` parameter, the architecture supports it through:
- **`min_p`** (line 214): Similar concept - minimum probability relative to max token probability
- **`logits_processors`**: Custom processors can implement additional sampling strategies
- **`extra_args`**: Dictionary for custom extension parameters

The `min_p` parameter is the closest built-in equivalent to Top-A, as both work by filtering tokens based on probability thresholds rather than counts (like `top_k`) or cumulative probability (like `top_p`).

---

**Files examined:**
- `/vllm/sampling_params.py` (603 lines, complete source)
- `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (598 lines, metadata structures)
- `/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` (2096 lines, API integration)
- `/tests/test_sampling_params.py` (validation tests)

| `best_of` | If set: must be int >= 1, and >= n |
| `presence_penalty` | Must be in [-2, 2] |
| `frequency_penalty` | Must be in [-2, 2] |
| `repetition_penalty` | Must be > 0 |
| `temperature` | Must be >= 0 |
| `top_p` | Must be in (0, 1] |
| `top_k` | Must be 0, -1, or >= 1; must be int |
| `min_p` | Must be in [0, 1] |
| `max_tokens` | If set, must be >= 1 |
| `min_tokens` | Must be >= 0, and <= max_tokens |
| `logprobs` | If set, must be >= 0 |
| `prompt_logprobs` | If set, must be >= 0 |
| `truncate_prompt_tokens` | Must be >= 1 |
| `stop_token_ids` | Must be list of ints only |
| `stop` | Cannot contain empty strings; requires detokenize=True |
| `best_of` vs `output_kind` | If best_of != n, cannot use DELTA output_kind |

### 4. **Special Validation Handling**

**In `__post_init__` (lines 327-385):**

1. **Temperature normalization**: If temperature < 1e-5, it's treated as zero (greedy sampling), and overrides are applied:
   - `top_p` → 1.0
   - `top_k` → 0
   - `min_p` → 0.0

2. **Temperature bounds checking**: If 0 < temperature < 1e-2, it's bumped up to 1e-2 to prevent numerical errors.

3. **Stop sequence normalization**:
   - Single stop string converted to list
   - Empty list if None

4. **Log probability conversion**: `True` values converted to `1`

5. **Seed handling**: `-1` seed converted to `None`

6. **Stop token tracking**: All stop tokens collected into `_all_stop_token_ids`

### 5. **Parameter Ranges and Defaults**

```python
_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5   # Epsilon for comparing floats to zero
_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2       # Minimum allowed temperature

# Default values (from ChatCompletionRequest):
temperature: 1.0
top_p: 1.0
top_k: 0
min_p: 0.0
repetition_penalty: 1.0
presence_penalty: 0.0
frequency_penalty: 0.0
```

### 6. **Key Methods**

- `from_optional()`: Factory method with optional parameters
- `__post_init__()`: Initialization validation and setup
- `_verify_args()`: Comprehensive parameter validation
- `_verify_greedy_sampling()`: Additional checks for greedy mode
- `update_from_generation_config()`: Updates from model config
- `update_from_tokenizer()`: Converts bad_words to token IDs
- `clone()`: Deep copy with special handling for LogitsProcessor
- `sampling_type` property: Returns SamplingType (GREEDY, RANDOM, RANDOM_SEED)

### 7. **Sampling Types** (from `SamplingType` enum)

```python
class SamplingType(IntEnum):
    GREEDY = 0           # temperature < 1e-5
    RANDOM = 1           # No seed
    RANDOM_SEED = 2      # With seed
```

### 8. **Relation to Your Top-A Interest**

While vLLM doesn't currently have a direct `top_a` parameter, the architecture supports it through:
- **`min_p`** (line 214): Similar concept - minimum probability relative to max token probability
- **`logits_processors`**: Custom processors can implement additional sampling strategies
- **`extra_args`**: Dictionary for custom extension parameters

The `min_p` parameter is the closest built-in equivalent to Top-A, as both work by filtering tokens based on probability thresholds rather than counts (like `top_k`) or cumulative probability (like `top_p`).

- `/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` (2096 lines, API integration)

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase to understand the sampler architecture. Here's my detailed findings:

### 1. **Key Files and Their Locations**

- `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - Main V0 sampler implementation with filtering logic
- `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` - V1 sampler implementation

### 2. **Sampling Parameters Definition** (`sampling_params.py`)

The `SamplingParams` class (lines 118-603) defines all sampling configuration:

```python
@dataclass
class SamplingParams(msgspec.Struct, omit_defaults=True, dict=True):
    # Key parameters:
    temperature: float = 1.0          # Temperature for sampling
    top_p: float = 1.0                # Cumulative probability threshold (0, 1]
    top_k: int = 0                    # Number of top tokens (0 or -1 = disabled)
    min_p: float = 0.0                # Minimum probability relative to max [0, 1]
    # ... other parameters like presence_penalty, frequency_penalty, etc.
```

**Important:** There is **no `top_a` parameter yet** in the codebase. This is where you'll add it.

---

### 3. **Data Structure Flow: SamplingMetadata & SamplingTensors**

The sampling pipeline uses two key data structures:

**SamplingMetadata** (`sampling_metadata.py`, lines 97-196):
- Contains sequence groups to sample (`seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample]`)
- Contains `selected_token_indices` - indices to extract from model output logits
- Contains `categorized_sample_indices` - indices grouped by sampling type (GREEDY, RANDOM, RANDOM_SEED)
- Used to organize which tokens need sampling

**SamplingTensors** (`sampling_metadata.py`, lines 372-598):
- Batched tensors created from SamplingMetadata for GPU processing
- Key fields:
  ```python
  @dataclass
  class SamplingTensors:
      temperatures: torch.Tensor
      top_ps: torch.Tensor
      top_ks: torch.Tensor
      min_ps: torch.Tensor
      presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
      frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
      repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
      prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
      output_tokens: torch.Tensor
  ```

**Critical:** This is where you'd add `top_as: torch.Tensor` field.

---

### 4. **Filtering Logic: top_p, top_k, min_p Implementation**

**V0 Sampler** (`model_executor/layers/sampler.py`):

The filtering functions are standalone functions that modify logits in-place:

**`_apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 398-424):**
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,      # top_p values (batch,)
    k: torch.Tensor,      # top_k values (batch,)
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # 1. Sort logits ascending (to identify which to filter)
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # 2. Apply top-k masking
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask                    # Boolean mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # 3. Apply top-p masking (cumulative probability)
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)                    # Cumulative sum
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)        # Boolean mask
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False                                # Keep at least one token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # 4. Restore original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(
        dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**`_apply_min_p()` (lines 427-441):**
```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,  # min_p values (batch,)
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # 1. Get probabilities
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    
    # 2. Find max probability per batch
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    
    # 3. Calculate threshold: min_p * max_probability
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    
    # 4. Filter tokens below threshold
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**V1 Sampler** (`v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`):

Similar implementation with `apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 168-208) and TPU-optimized variant.

---

### 5. **Sampling Flow in V0 Sampler** (`.forward()` method)

**Forward pass flow** (`model_executor/layers/sampler.py`, lines 225-345):

```python
def forward(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,        # (num_tokens, vocab_size)
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> SamplerOutput:
    
    # Step 1: Initialize sampling tensors from metadata
    self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
    sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
    do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
    do_min_p = self._do_min_p
    
    # Step 2: Apply penalties (frequency, presence, repetition)
    logits = apply_penalties(logits, ...)
    
    # Step 3: Apply temperature scaling
    logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    
    # Step 4: Apply top-k and top-p filtering
    if do_top_p_top_k:
        logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(
            logits, 
            sampling_tensors.top_ps,      # Shape: (batch,)
            sampling_tensors.top_ks       # Shape: (batch,)
        )
    
    # Step 5: Apply min-p filtering
    if do_min_p:
        logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)  # Shape: (batch,)
    
    # Step 6: Compute probabilities for sampling
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
    logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
    
    # Step 7: Sample tokens (multinomial or greedy)
    maybe_deferred_sample_results, _ = _sample(
        probs, logprobs, sampling_metadata, sampling_tensors, ...)
    
    # Return sampler output with token IDs
```

**Order of operations is critical:**
1. Penalties (don't affect probabilities much, just logits)
2. Temperature scaling
3. top-k/top-p filtering
4. min-p filtering
5. Softmax → probabilities
6. Sampling

---

### 6. **Key Design Patterns for Adding top_a**

Based on the `min_p` implementation, adding `top_a` would follow this pattern:

**Pattern similarity to min_p:**
- **min_p**: Filters tokens whose probability is less than `min_p * max_probability`
- **top_a**: Should filter tokens whose probability is less than `top_a * max_probability`

They're mathematically identical! The difference is semantic:
- `min_p` is typically smaller (e.g., 0.0 to 0.1)
- `top_a` might have different typical ranges (usually also small, e.g., 0.0 to 1.0)

---

   - Add validation in `_verify_args()` method: `0.0 <= top_a <= 1.0`
   - Update docstring

2. **`vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`:**

   - Add `top_as` to `from_sampling_metadata()` method logic
   - Add to tensor creation in `from_lists()` method

3. **`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`:**
   - Create new function `_apply_top_a()` (almost identical to `_apply_min_p()`)
   - Add `do_top_a` tracking flag in `_init_sampling_tensors()`
   - Add filtering call in `forward()` method after min_p
   - Add function call in temperature/penalty application flow

4. **`vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`:**
   - Add `top_a: Optional[torch.Tensor]` field to V1 `SamplingMetadata`

5. **`vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`:**
   - Create `apply_top_a()` function or integrate into existing function
   - Update sampler forward methods if needed

6. **Tests:**
   - Add test case in `tests/samplers/test_sampler.py`
   - Add test case in `tests/v1/sample/test_sampler.py`

---

### 8. **Implementation Template for _apply_top_a()**

Based on min_p implementation:

```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Apply top-a filtering: filters tokens whose probability is less than
    top_a * max_probability, where max_probability is the probability of
    the most likely token.
    
    Args:
        logits: (batch_size, vocab_size)
        top_a: (batch_size,) - top_a values
    
    Returns:
        Filtered logits with tokens below threshold set to -inf
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

### 9. **Data Flow Summary**

```
SamplingParams (top_a: float)
    ↓
SamplingMetadata.prepare()
    ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
    ├─ Collects top_a values from all seq_groups
    ├─ Creates tensor: top_as: torch.Tensor
    └─ Returns SamplingTensors with top_as field
    ↓
Sampler.forward()
    ├─ do_top_a = _do_top_a flag
    ├─ Calls _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)
    └─ Returns SamplerOutput with sampled tokens
```

---

### 10. **Important Implementation Notes**

1. **Shape Management:**
   - SamplingParams holds per-request parameters
   - SamplingTensors converts these to flat tensors of shape `(total_tokens_to_sample,)`
   - This flattening happens in `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()`

2. **In-place Operations:**
   - Filtering functions modify logits in-place with `masked_fill_()`
   - This is done for performance

3. **Boolean Masking:**
   - Tokens to remove are marked with `-float("inf")` so softmax makes them 0 probability
   - At least one token is always kept (by convention)

4. **min_p is Already Implemented:**
   - You can directly reference how min_p is done and replicate for top_a
   - They're mathematically equivalent

5. **Testing Strategy:**
   - Test that tokens below top_a threshold are filtered
   - Test that at least one token remains
   - Test with different top_a values (0.0, 0.5, 1.0)
   - Test interaction with other parameters

---

This architecture is clean and extensible. Adding `top_a` will be straightforward since it follows the exact same pattern as `min_p`.

    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

### 9. **Data Flow Summary**

```
SamplingParams (top_a: float)
    ↓
SamplingMetadata.prepare()
    ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
    ├─ Collects top_a values from all seq_groups
    ├─ Creates tensor: top_as: torch.Tensor
    └─ Returns SamplingTensors with top_as field
    ↓
Sampler.forward()
    ├─ do_top_a = _do_top_a flag
    ├─ Calls _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)
    └─ Returns SamplerOutput with sampled tokens
```

---

### 10. **Important Implementation Notes**

1. **Shape Management:**
   - SamplingParams holds per-request parameters
   - SamplingTensors converts these to flat tensors of shape `(total_tokens_to_sample,)`
   - This flattening happens in `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()`

2. **In-place Operations:**
   - Filtering functions modify logits in-place with `masked_fill_()`
   - This is done for performance

3. **Boolean Masking:**
   - Tokens to remove are marked with `-float("inf")` so softmax makes them 0 probability
   - At least one token is always kept (by convention)

4. **min_p is Already Implemented:**
   - You can directly reference how min_p is done and replicate for top_a
   - They're mathematically equivalent

5. **Testing Strategy:**
   - Test that tokens below top_a threshold are filtered
   - Test that at least one token remains
   - Test with different top_a values (0.0, 0.5, 1.0)
   - Test interaction with other parameters

---

This architecture is clean and extensible. Adding `top_a` will be straightforward since it follows the exact same pattern as `min_p`.

## Summary Report: vLLM Sampling Parameters Data Flow

- `temperature` (float, default 1.0): Randomness scaling
- `presence_penalty`, `frequency_penalty`, `repetition_penalty`: Token penalties
- `seed`: Random number generator seed for reproducibility

Each `SamplingParams` is validated during construction, particularly for parameter range validation (e.g., top_p must be in (0, 1]).

#### **SamplingMetadata** (V0 Pipeline)
**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478454924-g0vk54/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

Acts as a batched metadata container that groups sequences and identifies which tokens need sampling. Key components:

```python
@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:
    seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample]  # Batched sequence groups
    selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor      # Indices into logits for sampling
    categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor]  # Indices by type
    num_prompts: int
    skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool
    reuse_sampling_tensors: bool
```

The `SequenceGroupToSample` dataclass holds:
- `sampling_params: SamplingParams` - The original sampling configuration
- `sample_indices: list[int]` - Which logits positions to sample

#### **SamplingMetadata** (V1 Pipeline)
**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478454924-g0vk54/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`

A lighter structure optimized for the newer engine:
- `temperature`, `top_p`, `top_k`: Tensors (not individual values)
- `generators`: Dict of per-request generators
- `frequency_penalties`, `presence_penalties`, `repetition_penalties`: Tensors

- `allowed_token_ids_mask`, `bad_words_token_ids`: Filtering masks

#### **SamplingTensors**
**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478454924-g0vk54/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 372-597)

The critical transformation structure that converts per-sequence sampling params into batched tensors:

```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    temperatures: torch.Tensor        # Shape: (batch_size,)
    top_ps: torch.Tensor             # Shape: (batch_size,)
    top_ks: torch.Tensor             # Shape: (batch_size,) dtype=int

**Key transformation logic** (lines 386-495):
```python
@classmethod
def from_sampling_metadata(
    cls,
    sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
    vocab_size: int,
    device: torch.device,
    dtype: torch.dtype,
) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
```

This method:
1. **Extracts per-sequence parameters** from `sampling_metadata.seq_groups`
2. **Builds Python lists** of scalars (temperatures, top_p values, top_k values, etc.)
3. **Detects optimization flags**: 
   - `do_penalties`: Are any penalties active?
   - `do_top_p_top_k`: Are top-p or top-k filters needed?
   - `do_min_p`: Is min_p filtering needed?
4. **Converts to PyTorch tensors** with pinned memory for async H2D transfer
5. **Handles special cases**:
   - Top-k clamped to vocab_size
   - Zero temperature → set to 1.0 (greedy sampling)
   - Prompt logprob tokens: add temperature/penalties even when not sampling

### **2. Data Flow Through Sampling Pipeline**

#### **Phase 1: Preparation in Model Runner**
**Location:** `vllm/worker/model_runner.py` lines 1742-1745

```python

    seq_group_metadata_list, model_input.seq_lens,
    model_input.query_lens, self.device, self.pin_memory,
    generators, self.sampling_metadata_cache)
```

#### **Phase 2: Sampler Forward Pass**
**Location:** `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` lines 225-334

```python
def forward(self, logits: torch.Tensor, sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata):
    # 1. Initialize SamplingTensors from metadata
    (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
     do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
         sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
```

#### **Phase 3: Logits Transformation**

The sampler applies transformations in order:

1. **Min-tokens penalty** (lines 351-395): Penalize stop tokens if not enough tokens generated
2. **Penalty application** (lines 271-277): Presence, frequency, repetition penalties
   ```python
   logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                           sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                           sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                           sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                           sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
   ```

3. **Temperature scaling** (line 282):
   ```python
   logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
   ```

4. **Top-k and Top-p filtering** (lines 284-286):
   ```python
   if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
       logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                   sampling_tensors.top_ks)
   ```

5. **Min-p filtering** (lines 288-289):
   ```python
   if do_min_p:
       logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
   ```

#### **Phase 4: Top-k/Top-p Implementation**
**Location:** `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` lines 398-424 and `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

**V0 Implementation** (`_apply_top_k_top_p`):
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(logits: torch.Tensor,
                       p: torch.Tensor,      # top_p values per batch
                       k: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    # 1. Sort logits in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # 2. Apply top-k mask (set below threshold to -inf)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(logits_sort < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # 3. Apply top-p mask using cumulative probabilities
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # 4. Re-sort back to original vocab order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(...)
    return logits
```

**V1 Implementation** (`TopKTopPSampler`):
Multiple implementations based on hardware:
- **CUDA with FlashInfer** (lines 85-107): Uses optimized library calls
- **TPU** (lines 109-118): Avoids slow scatter operations
- **Native PyTorch** (lines 69-83): Portable implementation

#### **Phase 5: Min-p Implementation**
**Location:** `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` lines 427-441

```python
def _apply_min_p(logits: torch.Tensor,
                 min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    # 1. Compute probabilities
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    
    # 2. Find max probability per batch
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    
    # 3. Compute threshold: min_p * max_prob
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    
    # 4. Mask and return
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

#### **Phase 6: Probability Computation & Sampling**
**Location:** `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` lines 291-305

```python
probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)

# Dispatch to appropriate sampler based on sampling_type
maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
    probs, logprobs, sampling_metadata, sampling_tensors,
    include_gpu_probs_tensor=..., modify_greedy_probs=...)
```

The `_sample()` function (lines 738-765) routes to `_sample_with_torch()` which:

1. **Categorizes sequences by sampling type** (GREEDY, RANDOM, RANDOM_SEED)
2. **For GREEDY**: `torch.argmax(logprobs)`
3. **For RANDOM/RANDOM_SEED**: `_multinomial()` with optional per-request generators
4. **Returns Pythonized results** (GPU → CPU sync)

### **3. Penalty Application Details**

**Location:** `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` lines 30-63

```python
def apply_penalties(logits: torch.Tensor,
                   prompt_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,    # Padded prompts
                   output_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,    # Padded outputs
                   presence_penalties: torch.Tensor,
                   frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor,
                   repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    
    # Count token occurrences via scatter_add
    bin_counts = torch.zeros(...)
    bin_counts.scatter_add_(1, tokens, torch.ones_like(tokens))
    
    # Apply repetition penalty via custom op (prompt + output)
    apply_repetition_penalties(logits, prompt_mask, output_mask, ...)
    
    # Apply frequency penalty (output tokens only)
    logits -= frequency_penalties.unsqueeze(1) * output_bin_counts
    
    # Apply presence penalty (output tokens only)
    logits -= presence_penalties.unsqueeze(1) * output_mask
```

### **4. Key Files & Line References**

| Component | File | Lines | Purpose |
|-----------|------|-------|---------|
| **SamplingParams** | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | 118-589 | Input sampling configuration |
| **SamplingMetadata (V0)** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 97-195 | Batched sampling metadata |
| **SequenceGroupToSample** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 20-75 | Per-group sampling data |
| **SamplingTensors** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 372-597 | Batched tensor conversion |
| **Sampler.forward** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 225-334 | Main sampling orchestration |
| **_apply_top_k_top_p** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 398-424 | Top-k/top-p mask application |
| **_apply_min_p** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 427-441 | Min-p filtering |
| **apply_penalties** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` | 30-63 | Frequency/presence/repetition penalties |
| **TopKTopPSampler** | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | 22-297 | V1 sampling with hardware optimization |
| **apply_top_k_top_p (V1)** | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | 168-208 | V1 PyTorch implementation |
| **Model Runner** | `vllm/worker/model_runner.py` | 1742-1753 | SamplingMetadata preparation |

### **5. Test Coverage**

**Location:** Tests available for all major components:
- `tests/test_sampling_params.py` - SamplingParams validation and conversion
- `tests/samplers/test_sampler.py` - V0 sampler integration tests
- `tests/v1/sample/test_sampler.py` - V1 sampler with penalties and filters
- `tests/v1/sample/test_topk_topp_sampler.py` - Top-k/top-p correctness
- `tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py` - End-to-end sampling validation

### **6. Critical Design Patterns**

1. **Lazy Tensor Creation**: Tensors created on pinned memory for async H2D transfer
2. **Optimization Flags**: `do_penalties`, `do_top_p_top_k`, `do_min_p` skip unnecessary computation
3. **In-place Operations**: Many mask operations modify tensors in-place to save memory
4. **Hardware Specialization**: Different implementations for CUDA, TPU, CPU
5. **Batching Efficiency**: Parameters extracted once per batch, reused across sequences
6. **Deferred Pythonization**: GPU results can be kept as tensors for multi-step scenarios

This comprehensive pipeline ensures that sampling parameters from user requests flow efficiently through the entire system while supporting both V0 (stateless) and V1 (stateful) inference engines.

maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
    probs, logprobs, sampling_metadata, sampling_tensors,
    include_gpu_probs_tensor=..., modify_greedy_probs=...)
```

The `_sample()` function (lines 738-765) routes to `_sample_with_torch()` which:

1. **Categorizes sequences by sampling type** (GREEDY, RANDOM, RANDOM_SEED)
2. **For GREEDY**: `torch.argmax(logprobs)`
3. **For RANDOM/RANDOM_SEED**: `_multinomial()` with optional per-request generators
4. **Returns Pythonized results** (GPU → CPU sync)

### **3. Penalty Application Details**

**Location:** `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` lines 30-63

```python
def apply_penalties(logits: torch.Tensor,
                   prompt_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,    # Padded prompts
                   output_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,    # Padded outputs
                   presence_penalties: torch.Tensor,
                   frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor,
                   repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    
    # Count token occurrences via scatter_add
    bin_counts = torch.zeros(...)
    bin_counts.scatter_add_(1, tokens, torch.ones_like(tokens))
    
    # Apply repetition penalty via custom op (prompt + output)
    apply_repetition_penalties(logits, prompt_mask, output_mask, ...)
    
    # Apply frequency penalty (output tokens only)
    logits -= frequency_penalties.unsqueeze(1) * output_bin_counts
    
    # Apply presence penalty (output tokens only)
    logits -= presence_penalties.unsqueeze(1) * output_mask
```

### **4. Key Files & Line References**

| Component | File | Lines | Purpose |
|-----------|------|-------|---------|
| **SamplingParams** | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | 118-589 | Input sampling configuration |
| **SamplingMetadata (V0)** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 97-195 | Batched sampling metadata |
| **SequenceGroupToSample** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 20-75 | Per-group sampling data |
| **SamplingTensors** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 372-597 | Batched tensor conversion |
| **Sampler.forward** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 225-334 | Main sampling orchestration |
| **_apply_top_k_top_p** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 398-424 | Top-k/top-p mask application |
| **_apply_min_p** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 427-441 | Min-p filtering |
| **apply_penalties** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` | 30-63 | Frequency/presence/repetition penalties |
| **TopKTopPSampler** | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | 22-297 | V1 sampling with hardware optimization |
| **apply_top_k_top_p (V1)** | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | 168-208 | V1 PyTorch implementation |
| **Model Runner** | `vllm/worker/model_runner.py` | 1742-1753 | SamplingMetadata preparation |

### **5. Test Coverage**

**Location:** Tests available for all major components:
- `tests/test_sampling_params.py` - SamplingParams validation and conversion
- `tests/samplers/test_sampler.py` - V0 sampler integration tests
- `tests/v1/sample/test_sampler.py` - V1 sampler with penalties and filters
- `tests/v1/sample/test_topk_topp_sampler.py` - Top-k/top-p correctness
- `tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py` - End-to-end sampling validation

### **6. Critical Design Patterns**

1. **Lazy Tensor Creation**: Tensors created on pinned memory for async H2D transfer
2. **Optimization Flags**: `do_penalties`, `do_top_p_top_k`, `do_min_p` skip unnecessary computation
3. **In-place Operations**: Many mask operations modify tensors in-place to save memory
4. **Hardware Specialization**: Different implementations for CUDA, TPU, CPU
5. **Batching Efficiency**: Parameters extracted once per batch, reused across sequences
6. **Deferred Pythonization**: GPU results can be kept as tensors for multi-step scenarios

This comprehensive pipeline ensures that sampling parameters from user requests flow efficiently through the entire system while supporting both V0 (stateless) and V1 (stateful) inference engines.

32	class GuidedDecodingParams:
33	    """One of these fields will be used to build a logit processor."""
34	    json: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
35	    regex: Optional[str] = None
36	    choice: Optional[list[str]] = None
37	    grammar: Optional[str] = None
38	    json_object: Optional[bool] = None
39	    """These are other options that can be set"""
40	    backend: Optional[str] = None
41	    backend_was_auto: bool = False
42	    disable_fallback: bool = False
43	    disable_any_whitespace: bool = False
44	    disable_additional_properties: bool = False
45	    whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
46	    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None
47	
48	    @staticmethod
49	    def from_optional(
50	        json: Optional[Union[dict, BaseModel, str]] = None,
51	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
52	        choice: Optional[list[str]] = None,
53	        grammar: Optional[str] = None,
54	        json_object: Optional[bool] = None,
55	        backend: Optional[str] = None,
56	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
57	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
58	    ) -> Optional["GuidedDecodingParams"]:
59	        if all(arg is None for arg in (json, regex, choice, grammar,
60	                                       json_object, structural_tag)):
61	            return None
62	        # Extract json schemas from pydantic models
63	        if isinstance(json, (BaseModel, type(BaseModel))):
64	            json = json.model_json_schema()
65	        return GuidedDecodingParams(
66	            json=json,
67	            regex=regex,
68	            choice=choice,
69	            grammar=grammar,
70	            json_object=json_object,
71	            backend=backend,
72	            whitespace_pattern=whitespace_pattern,
73	            structural_tag=structural_tag,
74	        )
75	
76	    def __post_init__(self):
77	        """Validate that some fields are mutually exclusive."""
78	        guide_count = sum([
79	            self.json is not None, self.regex is not None, self.choice
80	            is not None, self.grammar is not None, self.json_object is not None
81	        ])
82	        if guide_count > 1:
83	            raise ValueError(
84	                "You can only use one kind of guided decoding but multiple are "
85	                f"specified: {self.__dict__}")
86	
87	        if self.backend is not None and ":" in self.backend:
88	            self._extract_backend_options()
89	
90	    @deprecated(
91	        "Passing guided decoding backend options inside backend in the format "
92	        "'backend:...' is deprecated. This will be removed in v0.10.0. Please "
93	        "use the dedicated arguments '--disable-fallback', "
94	        "'--disable-any-whitespace' and '--disable-additional-properties' "
95	        "instead.")
96	    def _extract_backend_options(self):
97	        """Extract backend options from the backend string."""
98	        assert isinstance(self.backend, str)
99	        self.backend, options = self.backend.split(":")
100	        options_set = set(options.strip().split(","))
101	        if "no-fallback" in options_set:
102	            self.disable_fallback = True
103	        if "disable-any-whitespace" in options_set:

109	class RequestOutputKind(Enum):
110	    # Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput
111	    CUMULATIVE = 0
112	    # Return only deltas in each RequestOutput
113	    DELTA = 1
114	    # Do not return intermediate RequestOutput
115	    FINAL_ONLY = 2

118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
161	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
164	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding

171	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
174	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
181	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
182	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
183	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
184	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special

186	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on

189	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size

192	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
193	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided

195	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor

197	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits

327	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
328	        # how we deal with `best_of``:
329	        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
330	        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
331	        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
332	        # when we return the result, we will check
333	        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
334	        if self.best_of:
335	            if self.best_of < self.n:
336	                raise ValueError(
337	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
338	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
339	            if not self._real_n:
340	                self._real_n = self.n
341	                self.n = self.best_of
342	
343	        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
344	            logger.warning(
345	                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
346	                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
347	                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
348	            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)
349	
350	        if self.seed == -1:
351	            self.seed = None
352	
353	        if self.stop is None:
354	            self.stop = []
355	        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
356	            self.stop = [self.stop]
357	
358	        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
359	            self.stop_token_ids = []
360	
361	        if self.bad_words is None:
362	            self.bad_words = []
363	
364	        if self.logprobs is True:
365	            self.logprobs = 1
366	
367	        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
